hero.description: Advising executive teams on technology, data and AI decisions with C-level execution depth from building systems, leading engineering teams, and delivering enterprise platforms with measurable outcomes.
hero.proofLine: Delivered enterprise data and AI platforms, led multidisciplinary teams, and enabled organizations to adopt data-driven decision-making at scale.

seo.profileDescription: Executive advisor in technology, data, and AI for leadership teams and boards. I help organizations move from strategy to measurable execution through enterprise-grade data and AI governance.
seo.twitterImageAlt: Martin Jurado Pedroza - Executive Advisor in technology, data and AI

about.label: About
about.title: Executive-Level Transformation Leadership
paragraphs: I work with executive teams and boards at the intersection of strategy and execution, helping organizations navigate technology transformation with clarity, alignment, and measurable impact. My approach combines deep technical insight with business acumen to ensure decisions are architected for both short-term wins and long-term value.

Rather than building products or features, I build organizational capability, mentoring leaders, designing governance structures, and crafting roadmaps that turn strategy into sustainable competitive advantage.

My advisory perspective is grounded in C-level execution: building resilient systems, leading multidisciplinary engineering organizations, and delivering data and AI platforms from architecture through production adoption.

I also drive organizational transformation through capability building: upskilling internal talent, establishing data and AI academies, and enabling business teams to adopt new practices with confidence and ownership.

faq.label: FAQ for decision makers
faq.title: Questions executives ask before engaging advisory
faq.intro: Short answers to the questions most boards and leadership teams ask when evaluating technology and AI advisory.

questions.question: How can you prove value in six months?
questions.answer: By combining a diagnostic sprint, prioritized roadmap, and three execution tracks: data readiness, model governance, and organizational capability. We define value metrics in the first 45 days and publish them in a measurable scorecard.

questions.question: How do you reduce transformation risk?
questions.answer: By splitting strategy into outcomes, risks, owners, and measurable milestones. I pair governance with technical architecture decisions so delivery teams and executive committees share one language and one source of truth.

questions.question: What does your advisory model deliver for the first quarter?
questions.answer: A clear transformation charter, decision framework, capability map, and operating model for AI and data adoption. The goal is to convert urgency into an executable plan with visible ownership and deadlines.

questions.question: Do you only advise on AI, or also business architecture?
questions.answer: Both. AI is effective when embedded in architecture, operations, incentives, and leadership cadence. I focus on end-to-end alignment from board priorities to engineering execution.

questions.question: Do you work with startups or only large enterprises?
questions.answer: I work across scales and complexity levels, from emerging growth teams to global organizations, but the delivery model is always adapted to leadership maturity and risk appetite.
